operators.label: Equals
operators.description: Property equals value. Strings use quoted JSON literals; numbers are unquoted.
operators.hintLine: Property == value. String: quote it — e.g. country == "US". Number: no quotes — e.g. level == 5.

operators.label: Not equals
operators.hintLine: Property != value. String: quote it — e.g. tier != "free". Number: no quotes — e.g. level != 0.

operators.label: Less than
operators.hintLine: Numbers only: property < value — e.g. age < 18 (unquoted). No string or alphabetical compare; string properties fail evaluation. Text → Equals.

operators.label: Less or equal
operators.hintLine: Numbers only: property <= value — e.g. score <= 100 (unquoted). No string compare; string properties fail evaluation. Text → Equals.

operators.label: Greater than
operators.hintLine: Numbers only: property > value — e.g. age > 18 (unquoted). No string or alphabetical compare; string properties fail evaluation. Text → Equals.

operators.label: Greater or equal
operators.hintLine: Numbers only: property >= value — e.g. level >= 5 (unquoted). No string compare; string properties fail evaluation. Text → Equals.

operators.label: In list
operators.description: Property value is one of the values in a JSON array.
operators.hintLine: Property must be in the value list — e.g. country in ["US","CA"].

operators.label: Not in list
operators.hintLine: Property must not be in the value list — e.g. env not in ["staging","dev"].

operators.label: List includes
operators.description: List/array property contains this value. Not substring search on a string.
operators.hintLine: List property must include this item — e.g. roles includes "premium". Not text substring.

operators.label: List excludes
operators.description: List/array property does not contain this value.
operators.hintLine: List property must not include this item — e.g. tags excludes "blocked".

operators.label: Text includes
operators.description: String property matches value with =~ (plain text; escaped when saved).
operators.hintLine: String property =~ value (plain text; saved as regex literal). E.g. email =~ @gmail.com.

operators.label: Text excludes
operators.description: String property does not match value with !~ (plain text; escaped when saved).
operators.hintLine: String property !~ value (plain text; saved as regex literal). E.g. user_agent !~ bot.

operators.label: Text matches pattern
operators.description: String property matches value with =~ (regex pattern in value). For plain text use Text includes.
operators.hintLine: String property =~ value (regex pattern). E.g. email =~ ".+@gmail\.com". Plain text → Text includes.

operators.label: Text does not match pattern
operators.description: String property does not match value with !~ (regex pattern in value). For plain text use Text excludes.
operators.hintLine: String property !~ value (regex pattern). E.g. user_agent !~ ".*bot.*". Plain text → Text excludes.
